name: Gut sulfide sensor (CstR + CRISPRa)
shortDescription: An ingestible sensor for hydrogen sulfide / persulfide, a gut-microbial metabolite linked to colitis and colorectal risk. The CstR persulfide-sensing repressor gates a CRISPRa circuit driving a fluorescent reporter, in the probiotic E. coli Nissle 1917.

input.detects: Luminal hydrogen sulfide / reactive sulfur species from gut microbial metabolism

sensing.sensorMechanism: CstR is a persulfide-sensing repressor; reactive sulfur species crosslink its cysteines and release it from its operator.
sensing.signalFlow: H2S/persulfide -> CstR releases Pcst -> transcribes an sgRNA -> dCas9-activator amplifies a fluorescent reporter (CRISPRa) -> fluorescence rises with gut sulfide.

parts.name: CstR regulator
parts.notes: Reactive-sulfur-species-responsive repressor; requires porting into E. coli Nissle.

parts.name: Reporter-activating sgRNA
parts.source: designed for CRISPRa upstream of a weak reporter promoter

parts.name: sfGFP reporter
parts.notes: Recoverable from stool for non-invasive readout.

output.result: Fluorescent-cell fraction rises with luminal sulfide.

performance.notes: CstR persulfide sensing and CRISPRa are validated separately; integrated device is a design template requiring CstR porting to Nissle.

safety.biocontainment: Probiotic E. coli Nissle host; add thyA/dapA auxotrophy for gut-restricted containment.
safety.notes: Probiotic chassis with a human-safety record; research / supervised clinical use only.

buildSteps.title: Port CstR + design sgRNA
buildSteps.description: Reconstitute CstR/Pcst in Nissle; design the CRISPRa sgRNA.

buildSteps.title: Assemble units
buildSteps.description: TU1: CstR. TU2: Pcst -> sgRNA. TU3: dCas9-omega. TU4: weak promoter -> sfGFP. Low-copy vector.

buildSteps.title: Validate in vitro
buildSteps.description: Confirm sulfide-donor response before any animal work.

buildSteps.title: Recover and measure
buildSteps.description: Recover cells from stool; quantify fluorescence.

provenance.source: Design template combining the CstR persulfide sensor with a dCas9 CRISPRa circuit in E. coli Nissle
provenance.license: Parts per their original sources; design template CC BY 4.0
